title: capsella bottnica
intro: Capsella bursa-pastoris, also known as the shepherds' purse, is a plant that belongs to the Brassicaceae family and is commonly found in lawns, meadows, and along roadsides. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of glucosinolates, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of consuming Capsella bursa-pastoris include its ability to soothe coughs, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions.
